questions.question: What is the purpose of a device special file in UNIX?
questions.hint: It is a file in the file system that represents a hardware device.

questions.question: How do device special files differ from regular files?
questions.hint: Device special files are initialized by the kernel and provide access to hardware devices, whereas regular files are initialized by the user and contain user data.

questions.question: What is the device number associated with the file /dev/null?
